enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions (AIFIs) - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions (AIFIs) - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into effect immediately upon issuance.
enriched_requirements.object: upon issuance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to All India Financial Institutions (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'AIFIs' and individually as a 'AIFI'), viz., Export-Import
Bank of India (EXIM Bank), National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development
(NABARD), National Housing Bank (NHB), Small Industries Development Bank
of India (SIDBI), and National Bank for Financing Infrastructure and Development
(NaBFID).
enriched_requirements.object: India Financial Institutions (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'AIFIs' and individually as a 'AIFI'), viz

Held for Trading (HFT) category;

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI's Board shall have the following responsibilities:
(1) The structure, design and contents of an AIFI's Internal Capital Adequacy
Assessment Process (ICAAP) should be approved by the Board of Directors to
ensure that the ICAAP forms an integral part of the management process and
decision -making culture of an AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: (1) The structure, design and contents of an AIFI's Internal Capital Adequacy Assessment Process (ICAAP) should b

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) An AIFI shall have an explicit, Board-approved capital plan which should spell
out the institution's objectives in regard to level of capital, the time horizon for
achieving those objectives, and in broad terms, the capital planning process and
the allocated responsibilities for that process.
enriched_requirements.object: capital plan which should spell out the institution's objectives in regard to level of capital, the time horizon for

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) An AIFI shall have a formal disclosure policy approved by the Board of Directors
that addresses the AIFI's approach for determining what disclosures it shall make
and the internal controls over the disclosure process.

The Board shall assess and document, at least once a year, whether the
processes relating to the ICAAP implemented by an AIFI successfully achieve
the objectives envisaged by the board.

Vehicle (SPV)' etc.

The PNCPS shall be perpetual, i.e., there is no maturity date and there are no

The rate of dividend payable to the investors shall be either a fixed rate or a
floating rate referenced to a market determined rupee interest benchmark rate.

all banking book exposures, which are exempted from capital market exposure
ceilings for direct investments / total capital market exposures.
enriched_requirements.object: all banking book exposures, which are exempted from capital market exposure ceilings for direct investments / total capital mark

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These
risk weights / capital charge shall also apply to exposures which are exempt from
'capital market exposure' limit.
enriched_requirements.object: which are exempt from 'capital market exposure' limit

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The exposure to capital instruments issued by NBFCs which are not deducted
and are required to be risk weighted in terms of paragraph 24(7)(ii)(b) shall be
risk weighted at 125 per cent or as per the external ratings, whichever is higher.
enriched_requirements.object: 125 per cent or as per the external ratings, whichever is higher

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The exposure to equity instruments issued by NBFCs which are not deducted
and are required to be risk weighted in terms of paragraph 24(7)(ii)(c) shall be
risk weighted at 250 per cent.
enriched_requirements.object: 250 per cent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All investments made by an AIFI in the paid-up equity of non-financial entities
(other than subsidiaries) made under their statutory mandate which exceed 49
per cent (for investments not made under an AIFI's statutory mandate, this limit
shall be 10%) of the issued common share capital of the issuing entity or where
the entity is an unconsolidated affiliate as defined in paragraph 24(7)(ii)(c)(i) shall
receive a risk weight of 1250 per cent.
enriched_requirements.object: issued common share capital of the issuing entity or where the entity is an unconsolidated affiliate as defined in paragraph 24(7)(ii)(

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Equity investments equal to or below 49
per cent paid-up equity of such investee companies shall be assigned a 125 per
cent risk weight or the risk weight as warranted by rating or lack of it, whichever
higher.
enriched_requirements.object: per cent risk weight or the risk weight as warranted by rating or lack of it, whichever higher

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The exposure to capital instruments issued by financial entities (other than a bank
and an NBFC) which are not deducted and are required to be risk weighted in
terms of paragraph 24(7)(ii)(b) shall be risk weighted at 125 per cent or as per
the external ratings whichever is higher.
enriched_requirements.object: 125 per cent or as per the external ratings whichever is higher

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The exposure to equity instruments
issued by financial entities (other than a bank and an NBFC) which are not
deducted and are required to be risk weighted in terms of paragraph 24(7)(ii)(c)
shall be risk weighted at 250 per cent.
enriched_requirements.object: 250 per cent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI's investments in the non-equity capital eligible instruments of a bank shall
be risk weighted as prescribed in paragraph 37.
enriched_requirements.object: prescribed in paragraph 37

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Loans and advances to an AIFI's own staff which are fully covered by
superannuation benefits and / or mortgage of flat / house shall attract a 20 per
cent risk weight.
enriched_requirements.object: cent risk weight

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Since flat / house is not an eligible collateral and since An AIFI
normally recover the dues by adjusting the superannuation benefits only at the
time of cessation from service, the concessional risk weight shall be applied
without any adjustment of the outstanding amount.
enriched_requirements.object: adjustment of the outstanding amount

conditions:
In case an AIFI is holding
eligible collateral in respect of amounts due from a staff member
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case an AIFI is holding
eligible collateral in respect of amounts due from a staff member, the outstanding
amount in respect of that staff member may be adjusted to the extent permissible,
as indicated in paragraph 149 to 157.
enriched_requirements.object: extent permissible, as indicated in paragraph 149 to 157

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Other loans and advances to AIFI's own staff shall be eligible for inclusion under
regulatory retail portfolio and shall therefore attract a 75 per cent risk weight.
enriched_requirements.object: under regulatory retail portfolio and shall therefore attract a 75 per cent risk weight

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: All other assets shall attract a uniform risk weight of 100 per cent.
enriched_requirements.object: weight of 100 per cent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The risk-weighted amount of an off-balance sheet item that
gives rise to credit exposure is generally calculated by means of a two-step
process:

conditions: Where the off-balance sheet item is secured by eligible collateral or guarantee
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where the off-balance sheet item is secured by eligible collateral or guarantee,
the credit risk mitigation guidelines detailed in paragraphs 146 to 173 may be
applied.

Non-market related off balance sheet items
(1) The credit equivalent amount in relation to a non-market related off-balance sheet
item like, direct credit substitutes, trade and performance related contingent items
and commitments with certain drawdown, other commitments, etc., shall be
determined by multiplying the contracted amount of that particular transaction by
the relevant credit conversion factor (CCF) as elaborated in Table 12.
enriched_requirements.object: the contracted amount of that particular transaction by the relevant credit conversion factor (CCF) as elaborated in Tabl

conditions:
Where the non-market related off-balance sheet item is an undrawn or partially
undrawn fund-based facility
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Where the non-market related off-balance sheet item is an undrawn or partially
undrawn fund-based facility, the amount of undrawn commitment to be included
in calculating the off-balance sheet non-market related credit exposures is the
maximum unused portion of the commitment that shall be drawn during the
remaining period to maturity.
enriched_requirements.object: remaining period to maturity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Treatment of total counterparty credit risk
(1) The total capital charge for counterparty credit risk shall cover the default risk as
well as credit migration risk of the counterparty reflected in mark-to-market losses
on the expected counterparty risk (such losses being known as credit value
adjustments, CVA).
enriched_requirements.object: as well as credit migration risk of the counterparty reflected in mark-to-market losses on the expected counterparty risk (such

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Counterparty risk may arise in the context of OTC
derivatives, exchange traded derivatives, and SFTs.
enriched_requirements.object: of OTC derivatives, exchange traded derivatives, and SFTs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The 'capital charge for default risk' shall be calculated using Current Exposure
Method (CEM) as explained in paragraph 77(2).
enriched_requirements.object: Exposure Method (CEM) as explained in paragraph 77(2)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The 'capital charge for CVA risk'
shall be calculated as explained in paragraph 77(3).
enriched_requirements.object: in paragraph 77(3)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notes:
Note - An AIFI shall calculate NGR on a counterparty-by-
counterparty basis for all transactions that are subject to legally
enforceable netting agreements
7 Inserted with effect from March 10, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions (AIFIs) -

enriched_requirements.object: counterparty-by- counterparty basis for all transactions that are subject to legally enforceable netting agreements 7 Inserted with

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The relevant risk-weight of the QCCP
shall apply to assets or collateral posted for other purposes.
enriched_requirements.object: collateral posted for other purposes

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation - The word 'custodian' may include a trustee, agent,
pledgee, secured creditor, or any other person that holds property in
a way that does not give such person a beneficial interest in such
property and will not result in such property being subject to legally
enforceable claims by such persons, creditors, or to a court-ordered
stay of the return of such property, should such person become
insolvent or bankrupt.
enriched_requirements.object: pledgee, secured creditor, or any other person that holds property in a way that does not give such person a beneficial inte

conditions:
where a CCP is to be considered as non-QCCP and the
exposure is to be reckoned on CCP
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) Exposures to non-qualifying CCPs
Note - In cases where a CCP is to be considered as non-QCCP and the
exposure is to be reckoned on CCP, the applicable risk weight shall be
according to the ratings assigned to the CCPs.
enriched_requirements.object: ratings assigned to the CCPs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For non-IMM banks the exposure should be discounted by
applying the factor: (1-exp(-0.05*Mi)) / (0.05*Mi);
enriched_requirements.object: the factor: (1-exp(-0

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Exposures to central counterparties arising from OTC derivatives
transactions, exchange traded derivatives transactions, and securities
financing transactions (SFTs) shall be subject to the counterparty credit risk
treatment as indicated in the paragraphs below.
enriched_requirements.object: counterparty credit risk treatment as indicated in the paragraphs below

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) For the purpose of capital adequacy framework, CCPs shall be considered as
financial institution.
enriched_requirements.object: institution

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, an AIFI's investments in the capital of CCPs
shall be guided in terms of paragraph 24.
enriched_requirements.object: of paragraph 24

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(v) Capital requirements shall be dependent on the nature of CCPs, viz.,
Qualifying CCPs (QCCPs) and non-Qualifying CCPs.
enriched_requirements.object: nature of CCPs, viz

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Under Pillar 2, an AIFI shall consider whether it may
need to hold capital in excess of the minimum capital requirements if,
for example, (i) its dealings with a CCP give rise to more risky
exposures, or (ii) where, given the context of that AIFI's dealings, it is
unclear that the CCP meets the definition of a QCCP.
enriched_requirements.object: need to hold capital in excess of the minimum capital requirements if, for example, (i) its dealings with a CCP give rise to m

conditions: if in the opinion of the Reserve Bank
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(b) An AIFI may be required to hold additional capital against its
exposures to QCCPs via Pillar 2, if in the opinion of the Reserve Bank,
it is necessary to do so.
enriched_requirements.object: additional capital against its exposures to QCCPs via Pillar 2

conditions: Where an AIFI is acting as a clearing member
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(c) Where an AIFI is acting as a clearing member, the AIFI shall assess,
through appropriate scenario analysis and stress testing, whether the
level of capital held against exposures to a CCP adequately addresses
the inherent risks of those transactions.
enriched_requirements.object: analysis and stress testing, whether the level of capital held against exposures to a CCP adequately addresses th

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
This assessment shall include
potential future or contingent exposures resulting from future drawings
on default fund commitments, and / or from secondary commitments
to take over or replace offsetting transactions from clients of another
clearing member in case of this clearing member defaulting or
becoming insolvent.
enriched_requirements.object: contingent exposures resulting from future drawings on default fund commitments, and / or from secondary commitments to ta

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(d) An AIFI shall monitor and report to senior management and the
appropriate committee of the Board (e.g., Risk Management
Committee) on a regular basis (quarterly or at more frequent intervals)
all of its exposures to CCPs, including exposures arising from trading
through a CCP and exposures arising from CCP membership
obligations such as default fund contributions.
enriched_requirements.object: senior management and the appropriate committee of the Board (e

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(e) Unless Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank requires otherwise,
the trades with a former QCCP may continue to be capitalised as
though they are with a QCCP for a period not exceeding three months
from the date it ceases to qualify as a QCCP.
enriched_requirements.object: as though they are with a QCCP for a period not exceeding three months from the date it ceases to qualify as a QCCP

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
After that time, an AIFI's
exposures with such a central counterparty shall be capitalised
according to rules applicable for non-QCCP.
enriched_requirements.object: rules applicable for non-QCCP

conditions:
Where an AIFI acts as a clearing member of a QCCP for its own
purposes
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(a) 8Where an AIFI acts as a clearing member of a QCCP for its own
purposes, a risk weight of 2 per cent shall be applied to the AIFI's trade
exposure to the QCCP in respect of OTC derivatives transactions,
exchange traded derivatives transactions, and SFTs.
enriched_requirements.object: AIFI's trade exposure to the QCCP in respect of OTC derivatives transactions, exchange traded derivatives transactions, and SFTs

conditions:
if it
is not obligated to reimburse the client for such losses
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, a clearing member (AIFI) is not required to maintain
capital for such transactions, for the trade exposure to the QCCP, if it
is not obligated to reimburse the client for such losses, provided the
AIFI obtains and maintains an independent, written, and reasoned
8 Substituted with effect from March 10, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions (AIFIs) -
Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Second Amendment Directions, 2026 dated March 10, 2026.
enriched_requirements.object: (AIFI) is not required to maintain capital for such transactions, for the trade exposure to the QCCP

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(b) The exposure amount for such trade exposure shall be calculated in
accordance with the CEM for derivatives and rules as applicable for
capital adequacy for Repo / Reverse Repo-style transactions.
enriched_requirements.object: with the CEM for derivatives and rules as applicable for capital adequacy for Repo / Reverse Repo-style transactions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(c) Where settlement is legally enforceable on a net basis in an event of
default and regardless of whether the counterparty is insolvent or
bankrupt, the total replacement cost of all contracts relevant to the
trade exposure determination shall be calculated as a net replacement
cost if the applicable close-out netting sets meet the requirements set
out in paragraph 79.
enriched_requirements.object: net replacement cost if the applicable close-out netting sets meet the requirements set out in paragraph 79

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(d) An AIFI shall have to demonstrate that the conditions mentioned
in paragraph 79 are fulfilled on a regular basis by obtaining
independent and reasoned legal opinion as regards legal certainty of
netting of exposures to QCCPs.
enriched_requirements.object: the conditions mentioned in paragraph 79 are fulfilled on a regular basis by obtaining independent and reasoned legal opinion

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall also obtain from the
QCCPs, the legal opinion taken by the respective QCCPs on the legal
certainty of their major activities such as settlement finality, netting,
collateral arrangements (including margin arrangements), default
procedures, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: QCCPs, the legal opinion taken by the respective QCCPs on the legal certainty of their major activities such as settlement finali

conditions: Where an AIFI is a client of the clearing member
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(e) Where an AIFI is a client of the clearing member, and enters into a
transaction with the clearing member acting as a financial intermediary
(i.e., the clearing member completes an offsetting transaction with a
QCCP), the client's exposures to the clearing member shall receive
the treatment prescribed in clause (i) above, provided the following
conditions are met:
enriched_requirements.object: in clause (i) above

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The client AIFI shall obtain an independent, written, and
reasoned legal opinion that concludes that, in the event of legal
challenge, the relevant courts and administrative authorities
would find that the client would bear no losses on account of the
insolvency of an intermediary under the relevant law, including:
enriched_requirements.object: and reasoned legal opinion that concludes that, in the event of legal challenge, the relevant courts and administrativ

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Relevant laws, regulations, rules, contractual, or administrative
arrangements provide that the offsetting transactions with the
defaulted or insolvent clearing member are highly likely to
continue to be indirectly transacted through the QCCP, or by the
QCCP, should the clearing member default or become insolvent.
enriched_requirements.object: or become insolvent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In such circumstances, the client positions and collateral with the
QCCP shall be transferred at the market value unless the client
requests to close out the position at the market value.
enriched_requirements.object: market value unless the client requests to close out the position at the market value

conditions: if relevant laws
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In this
context, it is clarified that if relevant laws, regulations, rules,
contractual, or administrative agreements provide that trades are
highly likely to be ported, this condition shall be considered to be
met.
enriched_requirements.object: met

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If there is a clear precedent for transactions being ported at
a QCCP and intention of the participants is to continue this
practice, then these factors shall be considered while assessing
if trades are highly likely to be ported.
enriched_requirements.object: if trades are highly likely to be ported

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(f) Where a client is not protected from losses in the case that the clearing
member and another client of the clearing member jointly default or
become jointly insolvent, but all other conditions mentioned above are
met and the concerned CCP is a QCCP, a risk weight of 4 per cent
shall apply to the client's exposure to the clearing member.
enriched_requirements.object: exposure to the clearing member

conditions:
Where a client AIFI does not meet the requirements in the above
paragraphs
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(g) Where a client AIFI does not meet the requirements in the above
paragraphs, an AIFI shall be required to capitalise its exposure
(including potential CVA risk exposure) to the clearing member as a
bilateral trade.
enriched_requirements.object: its exposure (including potential CVA risk exposure) to the clearing member as a bilateral trade

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(h) Under situations in which a client enters into a transaction with the
QCCP with a clearing member guaranteeing its performance, the
capital requirements shall be based on the provisions herein.
enriched_requirements.object: provisions herein

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) In all cases, any assets or collateral posted shall, from the perspective
of an AIFI posting such collateral, receive the risk weights that
otherwise applies to such assets or collateral under the capital
adequacy framework, regardless of the fact that such assets have
been posted as collateral.
enriched_requirements.object: of an AIFI posting such collateral, receive the risk weights that otherwise applies to such assets or collateral under the capi

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Thus, collateral posted from Banking Book
shall receive Banking Book treatment and collateral posted from
Trading Book shall receive Trading Book treatment.
enriched_requirements.object: and collateral posted from Trading Book shall receive Trading Book treatment

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where assets or
collateral of a clearing member or client are posted with a QCCP or a
clearing member and are not held in a bankruptcy remote manner, the
AIFI posting such assets or collateral shall also recognise credit risk
based upon the assets or collateral being exposed to risk of loss based
on the creditworthiness of the entity holding such assets or collateral.
enriched_requirements.object: based upon the assets or collateral being exposed to risk of loss based on the creditworthiness of the entity holding such

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall develop, implement and improve
systems for tracking and monitoring the credit risk exposure arising from
unsettled transactions as appropriate for producing management information that
facilitates action on a timely basis.
enriched_requirements.object: systems for tracking and monitoring the credit risk exposure arising from unsettled transactions as appropriate for pro

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) An AIFI shall closely monitor securities and foreign exchange transactions that
have failed, starting from the day they fail for producing management information
that facilitates action on a timely basis.
enriched_requirements.object: foreign exchange transactions that have failed, starting from the day they fail for producing management information th

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, a capital charge is required for
failed transactions and shall be calculated as under.

conditions:
If the payments have not yet taken place five business
days after the settlement date
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) For DvP Transactions - If the payments have not yet taken place five business
days after the settlement date, an AIFI shall calculate a capital charge by
multiplying the positive current exposure of the transaction by the appropriate
factor as provided Table 15.
enriched_requirements.object: by multiplying the positive current exposure of the transaction by the appropriate factor as provided Table 15

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In order to capture the information, an AIFI shall
need information system that can track the number of days after the agreed
settlement date and calculate the corresponding capital.
enriched_requirements.object: can track the number of days after the agreed settlement date and calculate the corresponding capital

(5) For non-DvP transactions (free deliveries) after the first contractual payment /
delivery leg, an AIFI that has made the payment shall treat its exposure as a loan
if the second leg has not been received by the end of the business day.
enriched_requirements.object: a loan if the second leg has not been received by the end of the business day

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Requirements for recognition of net replacement cost in close-out netting sets
(1) For repo-style transactions
Note - The holding period for the haircuts shall depend as in other repo-
style transactions on the frequency of margining
(2) For derivatives transactions
enriched_requirements.object: repo- style transactions on the frequency of margining (2) For derivatives transactions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) The effects of bilateral netting agreements covering repo-style transactions
shall be recognised on a counterparty-by-counterparty basis if the
agreements are legally enforceable in each relevant jurisdiction upon the
occurrence of an event of default and regardless of whether the counterparty
is insolvent or bankrupt.
enriched_requirements.object: counterparty-by-counterparty basis if the agreements are legally enforceable in each relevant jurisdiction upon the occurrence of a

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: In addition, netting agreements shall:

(e) Netting across positions in the banking book and trading book shall
only be recognised when the netted transactions fulfil the following
conditions:
enriched_requirements.object: the netted transactions fulfil the following conditions:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall maintain capital against all securitisation exposure amounts,
including those arising from the provision of credit risk mitigants to a securitisation
transaction, investments in asset-backed or mortgage-backed securities,
retention of a subordinated tranche, and extension of a liquidity facility or credit
enhancement.
enriched_requirements.object: securitisation exposure amounts, including those arising from the provision of credit risk mitigants to a securitisation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For capital computation, whenever securitisation exposures are a
subject of repurchase agreements and repurchased by an AIFI, the exposure
shall be treated as retained exposure and not a fresh exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: exposure and not a fresh exposure

conditions: where recognised upfront
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall
deduct from CET 1 any increase in equity capital resulting from a securitisation
transaction, either realised at the time of sale of underlying assets to the SPE, or
unrealised gains on sale of underlying assets such as that associated with
expected future margin income, where recognised upfront, till the maturity of such
assets.
enriched_requirements.object: any increase in equity capital resulting from a securitisation transaction, either realised at the time of sale of underlying assets to the SPE, or unrealised gains on sale of underlying assets such as that associated with expected future margin income

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For calculating exposure amount, an AIFI shall measure the exposure amount of
its off-balance exposure as follows:
enriched_requirements.object: of its off-balance exposure as follows:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) for credit risk mitigants sold or purchased by an AIFI, the treatment set out
in paragraphs 146 to 173 shall apply;

(ii) for facilities that are not eligible credit risk mitigants, the AIFI shall use a
CCF of 100 per cent;
enriched_requirements.object: 100 per cent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) for derivatives contracts other than credit risk derivatives contracts, such as
interest rate or currency swaps sold or purchased by the AIFI, to the extent
not covered by paragraphs 81(i) and 81(ii) above, the measurement
approach set out in paragraph 77(2) the dated November 10, 2016 on
'Guidelines for computing exposure for counterparty credit risk arising from
derivative transactions' shall apply.

If an AIFI can
verify that fulfilling its obligations with respect to exposure A shall preclude a loss
from its exposure to B under any circumstance, the AIFI does not need to
calculate risk-weighted assets for its exposure B.
enriched_requirements.object: its exposure to B under any circumstance, the AIFI does not need to calculate risk-weighted assets for its exposure B

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
To arrive at an overlap, an AIFI shall, for the purposes of calculating capital
requirements, split or expand its exposures, i.e., splitting exposures into portions
that overlap with another exposure held by the AIFI and other portions that do
not overlap;
enriched_requirements.object: of calculating capital requirements, split or expand its exposures, i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For example, a liquidity facility shall not be
contractually required to cover defaulted assets in certain circumstances.
enriched_requirements.object: cover defaulted assets in certain circumstances

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For
capital purposes, such a situation shall not be regarded as an overlap to the
securitisation notes issued by that securitisation.
enriched_requirements.object: overlap to the securitisation notes issued by that securitisation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, the AIFI shall
calculate RWAs for the liquidity facility as if it were expanded (either to cover

securitisation notes.
enriched_requirements.object: liquidity facility as if it were expanded (either to cover defaulted assets or in terms of trigger events) to preclude all loss

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In such a case, the AIFI shall only need to calculate capital
requirements on the liquidity facility.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Overlap may also be recognised between relevant capital charges for exposures
in the trading book and capital charges for exposures in the banking book,

provided that the AIFI is able to calculate and compare the capital charges for
the relevant exposures.
enriched_requirements.object: to calculate and compare the capital charges for the relevant exposures

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Liquidity facilities provided by an AIFI that do not satisfy the requirements of
Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Securitisation
Transactions) Directions, 2025 shall maintain capital charge equal to the actual
exposure, after applying a CCF of 100 per cent for the undrawn portion.
enriched_requirements.object: to the actual exposure, after applying a CCF of 100 per cent for the undrawn portion

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All securitisation exposures, which are not covered by these directions, or which
do not satisfy the conditions prescribed in these directions (including the
exposures prohibited and conditions prescribed as per Reserve Bank of India (All
India Financial Institutions - Securitisation Transactions) Directions, 2025 or
where originator is not a lender referred to in Reserve Bank of India (All India

prudential treatment is not advised explicitly in these directions or Reserve Bank
of India (All India Financial Institutions - Securitisation Transactions) Directions,
2025, an AIFI shall maintain capital charge equal to the actual exposure and shall
be subjected to supervisory scrutiny and suitable action.
enriched_requirements.object: per Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Securitisation Transactions) Directions, 2025 or where orig

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An originator shall maintain capital against the exposures transferred to a SPE,
which then forms the underlying for securitisation notes issued by the SPE, i.e.,
the exposures transferred to a SPE shall be included in the calculation of risk-
weighted assets of the originator and the consideration received from SPE shall
be recognised as an advance, unless the following conditions are satisfied.
enriched_requirements.object: exposures transferred to a SPE, which then forms the underlying for securitisation notes issued by the SPE, i.e., the exposures transferred to a SPE shall be included in the calculation of risk- weighted assets of the originator and the consideration received from SPE shall be recognised as an advance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Explanation - For this paragraph, retention of servicing rights in respect of the
transferred exposures shall not constitute control by the originator over the
transferred exposures.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

If this condition is not satisfied, then the
amount of credit protection to be recognised shall be computed as indicated
in sub-paragraph (3)(ii) below.
enriched_requirements.object: in sub-paragraph (3)(ii) below

conditions:
If the conditions (i) and (ii) above are not satisfied or an AIFI breaches any of
these conditions subsequently
enriched_requirements.object: the underlying asset

conditions: where it shall be subject to specific risk
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and the CDS position shall be transferred to Trading Book
where it shall be subject to specific risk, counterparty credit risk and general
market risk (wherever applicable) capital requirements as applicable to Trading
Book.
enriched_requirements.object: Book where it shall be subject to specific risk, counterparty credit risk and general market risk (wherever applicable) capi

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The unprotected portion of the underlying exposure shall be risk-weighted as
applicable under the Standardised Approach for credit risk.
enriched_requirements.object: under the Standardised Approach for credit risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The amount of credit
protection shall be adjusted if there are any mismatches between the underlying
asset / obligation and the reference / deliverable asset / obligation with regard to
asset or maturity.
enriched_requirements.object: are any mismatches between the underlying asset / obligation and the reference / deliverable asset / obligation with regard to as

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Protection shall be
reckoned as available by the protection buyer only if the mismatched assets
meet the requirements that (1) the reference obligation or deliverable
obligation ranks pari passu with or is junior to the underlying obligation, and
(2) the underlying obligation and reference obligation or deliverable obligation
share the same obligor (i.e., the same legal entity) and legally enforceable
cross-default or cross-acceleration clauses are in place.
enriched_requirements.object: by the protection buyer only if the mismatched assets meet the requirements that (1) the reference obligation or deliverable

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Maturity Mismatches: The protection buyer shall be eligible to reckon the
amount of protection if the maturity of the credit derivative contract were to be
equal or more than the maturity of the underlying asset.
enriched_requirements.object: the amount of protection if the maturity of the credit derivative contract were to be equal or more than the maturity of the und

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If, however, the
maturity of the CDS contract is less than the maturity of the underlying asset,
then it shall be construed as a maturity mismatch.
enriched_requirements.object: maturity mismatch

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case of maturity
mismatch the amount of protection shall be determined in the following
manner:
enriched_requirements.object: following manner:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(a) If the residual maturity of the credit derivative product is less than three
months no protection shall be recognised.

(b) If the residual maturity of the credit derivative contract is three months
or more protection proportional to the period for which it is available
shall be recognised.

(c) When there is a maturity mismatch the following adjustment shall be
applied.

Where such third party protection is purchased and is recognised as a
hedge of a Banking Book exposure for regulatory capital purposes, no capital
shall be maintained on internal and external CDS hedge.
enriched_requirements.object: and external CDS hedge

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In such cases, the
external CDS will act as indirect hedge for the Banking Book exposure and the
capital adequacy in terms of paragraphs 122, as applicable for external / third
party hedges, shall be applicable.

For all CDS positions including the hedged positions, both in the
Banking Book and Trading Book, banks should hold general provisions for gross
positive marked-to-market values of the CDS contracts.
enriched_requirements.object: gross positive marked-to-market values of the CDS contracts

conditions:
In case the credit event payment is not received within the period as
stipulated in the CDS contract
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Protection Buyer
In case the credit event payment is not received within the period as
stipulated in the CDS contract, the protection buyer shall ignore the credit
protection of the CDS and reckon the credit exposure on the underlying
<image>
asset and maintain appropriate level of capital and provisions as warranted
for the exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: of the CDS and reckon the credit exposure on the underlying <image> asset and maintain appropriate level of capital and p

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
On receipt of the credit event payment, (a) the underlying
asset shall be removed from the books if it has been delivered to the
protection seller or (b) the book value of the underlying asset shall be
reduced to the extent of credit event payment received if the credit event
payment does not fully cover the book value of the underlying asset and
appropriate provisions shall be maintained for the reduced value.
enriched_requirements.object: books if it has been delivered to the protection seller or (b) the book value of the underlying asset shall be reduced to the exte

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(a) From the date of credit event and until the credit event payment in
accordance with the CDS contract, the protection seller shall debit the
Profit and Loss account and recognise a liability to pay to the
protection buyer, for an amount equal to fair value of the contract
(notional of credit protection less expected recovery value).
enriched_requirements.object: Loss account and recognise a liability to pay to the protection buyer, for an amount equal to fair value of the contract (notiona

conditions:
in case of cash settlement) is not
available after the date of the credit event
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case,
the fair value of the deliverable obligation (in case of physical
settlement) / reference obligation (in case of cash settlement) is not
available after the date of the credit event, then until the time that value
is available, the protection seller should debit the Profit and Loss
account for the full amount of the protection sold and recognise a
liability to pay to the protection buyer equal to that amount.
enriched_requirements.object: Loss account for the full amount of the protection sold and recognise a liability to pay to the protection buyer equal to that am

conditions: In case of physical settlement
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(b) In case of physical settlement, after the credit event payment, the
protection seller shall recognise the assets received, if any, from the
protection buyer at the fair value.

Thereafter, the
protection seller shall subject these assets to the appropriate
prudential treatment as applicable to corporate bonds.
enriched_requirements.object: the appropriate prudential treatment as applicable to corporate bonds

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
It should, therefore, not be used as a substitute for a
bank guarantee.
enriched_requirements.object: used as a substitute for a bank guarantee

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, a bank should not sell credit protection by
writing a CDS on a corporate bond on the date of its issuance in the primary
<image>
market or undertake, before or at the time of issuance of the bonds, to write
such protection in future.
enriched_requirements.object: writing a CDS on a corporate bond on the date of its issuance in the primary <image> market or undertake, before or at the t

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall use
the ratings of the following domestic credit rating agencies (arranged in
alphabetical order) for the purposes of risk weighting its claims for capital
adequacy purposes:
enriched_requirements.object: the following domestic credit rating agencies (arranged in alphabetical order) for the purposes of risk weighting its claims for ca

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI may also use the ratings of the following international credit rating
agencies (arranged in alphabetical order) for the purposes of risk weighting its
claims for capital adequacy purposes where specified:
enriched_requirements.object: of the following international credit rating agencies (arranged in alphabetical order) for the purposes of risk weighting its cla

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall use the chosen credit rating agency and its ratings consistently for
each type of claim, for both risk weighting and risk management purposes.
enriched_requirements.object: rating agency and its ratings consistently for each type of claim, for both risk weighting and risk management purposes

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An
AIFI shall not 'cherry pick' the assessments provided by different credit rating
agencies and arbitrarily change the use of credit rating agency.
enriched_requirements.object: provided by different credit rating agencies and arbitrarily change the use of credit rating agency

conditions:
If an AIFI has
decided to use the ratings of some of the chosen credit rating agencies for a
given type of claim
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If an AIFI has
decided to use the ratings of some of the chosen credit rating agencies for a
given type of claim, it shall use only the ratings of those credit rating agency,
despite the fact that some of these claims may also be rated by other chosen
credit rating agency whose ratings the AIFI has decided not to use.
enriched_requirements.object: of those credit rating agency, despite the fact that some of these claims may also be rated by other chosen credit rating agency

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall
not use one agency's rating for one corporate bond, while using another agency's
rating for another exposure to the same counterparty, unless the respective
exposures are rated by only one of the chosen credit rating agencies, whose
ratings the AIFI has decided to use.
enriched_requirements.object: for one corporate bond, while using another agency's rating for another exposure to the same counterparty

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
External assessments for one entity within a
corporate group shall not be used to risk weight other entities within the same
group.
enriched_requirements.object: weight other entities within the same group

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall disclose the names of all the credit rating agencies it uses for the
risk weighting of its assets, the risk weights associated with the particular rating
grades as determined by the Reserve Bank through the mapping process for
each eligible credit rating agency as well as the aggregated RWAs as required
vide Table DF-4 of Annex III.
enriched_requirements.object: all the credit rating agencies it uses for the risk weighting of its assets, the risk weights associated with the particular rat

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
To be eligible for risk-weighting purposes, the external credit assessment shall
take into account and reflect the entire amount of credit risk exposure an AIFI has
with regard to all payments owed to it.
enriched_requirements.object: reflect the entire amount of credit risk exposure an AIFI has with regard to all payments owed to it

conditions:
if an AIFI is owed both
principal and interest
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For example, if an AIFI is owed both
principal and interest, the assessment shall fully take into account and reflect the
credit risk associated with timely repayment of both principal and interest.
enriched_requirements.object: and reflect the credit risk associated with timely repayment of both principal and interest

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
To be eligible for risk weighting purposes, the rating must be in force and
confirmed from the monthly bulletin of the concerned rating agency.
enriched_requirements.object: confirmed from the monthly bulletin of the concerned rating agency

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The rating
agency should have reviewed the rating at least once during the previous 15
months.
enriched_requirements.object: at least once during the previous 15 months

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An eligible credit assessment shall be publicly available i.e., a rating shall be
published in an accessible form and included in the external credit rating agency's
<image>
transition matrix.

Consequently, a rating that is made available only to the parties
to a transaction shall not satisfy this requirement.

For an asset in an AIFI's portfolio that has contractual maturity less than or equal
to one-year, short term ratings accorded by the chosen credit rating agencies
shall be relevant.

For other asset which has a contractual maturity of more than
one-year, long term ratings accorded by the chosen credit rating agencies shall
be relevant.

Cash credit exposure, even though, sanctioned for period of one year or less,
shall be reckoned as long-term exposures and accordingly the long-term ratings
accorded by the chosen credit rating agency shall be relevant.
enriched_requirements.object: exposures and accordingly the long-term ratings accorded by the chosen credit rating agency shall be relevant

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly, an AIFI
(if permitted to extend cash credit facility) may use long-term ratings of a
counterparty as a proxy for an unrated short-term exposure on the same
counterparty subject to strict compliance with the requirements for use of multiple
rating assessments and applicability of issue rating to issuer / other claims as
indicated in paragraphs 134 to 148.
enriched_requirements.object: a counterparty as a proxy for an unrated short-term exposure on the same counterparty subject to strict compliance with the r

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The mapping
process is required to result in a risk weight assignment consistent with that of
the level of credit risk.
enriched_requirements.object: risk weight assignment consistent with that of the level of credit risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A mapping of the credit ratings awarded by the chosen
domestic credit rating agency has been furnished below in paragraphs 134 and
140, which shall be used by an AIFI in assigning risk weights to the various
exposures.
enriched_requirements.object: AIFI in assigning risk weights to the various exposures

by an AIFI in India:

conditions: Where '+' or '-' notation is attached to the rating
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where '+' or '-' notation is attached to the rating, the corresponding main rating
category risk weight shall be used.

For example, A+ or A- shall be in the A rating
category and assigned 50 per cent risk weight.
enriched_requirements.object: rating category and assigned 50 per cent risk weight

conditions:
If an issuer has a long-term exposure with an external long-term rating that
warrants a risk weight of 150 per cent
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If an issuer has a long-term exposure with an external long-term rating that
warrants a risk weight of 150 per cent, all unrated claims on the same
counterparty, whether short-term or long-term, shall also receive a 150 per cent
risk weight, unless an AIFI uses recognised credit risk mitigation techniques for
such claims.
enriched_requirements.object: per cent risk weight

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
They shall only be used to derive risk weights for claims arising from the rated
facility.
enriched_requirements.object: derive risk weights for claims arising from the rated facility

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: They shall not be generalised to other short-term claims.
enriched_requirements.object: short-term claims

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In no event a
short-term rating shall be used to support a risk weight for an unrated long-term
claim.
enriched_requirements.object: a risk weight for an unrated long-term claim

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Short-term assessments may only be used for short-term claims against a
bank and corporate.
enriched_requirements.object: short-term claims against a bank and corporate

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the above restriction on using an issue specific short-term rating
for other short-term exposures, the following broad principles shall apply.

The
unrated short-term claim on counterparty shall attract a risk weight of at least one
level higher than the risk weight applicable to the rated short-term claim on that
counterparty.
enriched_requirements.object: of at least one level higher than the risk weight applicable to the rated short-term claim on that counterparty

conditions:
If a short-term rated facility to counterparty attracts a 20 per cent or
a 50 per cent risk-weight
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If a short-term rated facility to counterparty attracts a 20 per cent or
a 50 per cent risk-weight, unrated short-term claims to the same counterparty
shall not attract a risk weight lower than 30 per cent or 100 per cent respectively.
enriched_requirements.object: lower than 30 per cent or 100 per cent respectively

conditions:
if an issuer has a short-term exposure with an external short-term rating
that warrants a risk weight of 150 per cent
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly, if an issuer has a short-term exposure with an external short-term rating
that warrants a risk weight of 150 per cent, all unrated claims on the same
counterparty, whether long-term or short-term, shall also receive a 150 per cent
risk weight, unless an AIFI uses recognised credit risk mitigation techniques for
such claims.
enriched_requirements.object: per cent risk weight

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of the issue specific short-term ratings the following risk weight
mapping shall be adopted by an AIFI:
Table 23: Risk weight mapping of short-term ratings of
domestic rating agencies

Brickwork

Private Limited

INFOMERICS
Research Ltd.
enriched_requirements.object: AIFI: Table 23: Risk weight mapping of short-term ratings of domestic rating agencies India Ratings and Brickwork Ratings Standardise

conditions: Where '+' or '-' notation is attached to the rating
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where '+' or '-' notation is attached to the rating, the corresponding main rating
category risk weight shall be used for A2 and below, unless specified otherwise.
enriched_requirements.object: and below

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For example, A2+ or A2- shall be in the A2 rating category and assigned 50 per
cent risk weight.
enriched_requirements.object: rating category and assigned 50 per cent risk weight

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The above risk weight mapping of both long term and short-term ratings of the
chosen domestic rating agencies shall be reviewed annually by the Reserve
Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A rating shall be treated as solicited only if the issuer of the instrument has
requested the credit rating agency for the rating and has accepted the rating
assigned by the agency.
enriched_requirements.object: only if the issuer of the instrument has requested the credit rating agency for the rating and has accepted the rating assigne

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall use only solicited rating from the chosen
credit rating agencies.
enriched_requirements.object: from the chosen credit rating agencies

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
No ratings issued by the credit rating agency on an
unsolicited basis shall be considered for risk weight calculation as per the
standardised approach.
enriched_requirements.object: weight calculation as per the standardised approach

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall be guided by the following in respect of exposures / obligors having
multiple ratings from the chosen credit rating agency chosen by an AIFI for the
purpose of risk weight calculation:
enriched_requirements.object: following in respect of exposures / obligors having multiple ratings from the chosen credit rating agency chosen by an AIFI for the p

conditions:
If there is only one rating by a chosen credit rating agency for a particular
claim
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) If there is only one rating by a chosen credit rating agency for a particular
claim, that rating shall be used to determine the risk weight of the claim.
enriched_requirements.object: the risk weight of the claim

conditions:
If there are two ratings accorded by chosen credit rating agencies that map
into different risk weights
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) If there are two ratings accorded by chosen credit rating agencies that map
into different risk weights, the higher risk weight shall be applied.

conditions:
If there are three or more ratings accorded by chosen credit rating agencies
with different risk weights
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) If there are three or more ratings accorded by chosen credit rating agencies
with different risk weights, the ratings corresponding to the two lowest risk
weights shall be referred to and the higher of those two risk weights shall
be applied.
enriched_requirements.object: the higher of those two risk weights shall be applied

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where an AIFI invests in a particular issue that has an issue specific rating by a
chosen credit rating agency the risk weight of the claim shall be based on this
assessment.
enriched_requirements.object: assessment

conditions:
Where an AIFI's claim is not an investment in a specific assessed
issue

where a short-term claim on a counterparty is rated as A1+
and a long-term claim on the same counterparty is rated as AAA
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where an AIFI's claim is not an investment in a specific assessed
issue, the following general principles shall apply:
Note: In a case where a short-term claim on a counterparty is rated as A1+
and a long-term claim on the same counterparty is rated as AAA, then an
AIFI shall assign a 30 per cent risk weight to an unrated short-term claim
and 20 per cent risk weight to an unrated long-term claim on that
counterparty where the seniority of the claim ranks pari-passu with the rated
claims and the maturity of the unrated claim is not later than the rated claim.
enriched_requirements.object: case where a short-term claim on a counterparty is rated as A1+ and a long-term claim on the same counterparty is rated as AAA, then

conditions:
where a short-term claim is rated A1+ and a long-term
claim is rated A
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In a similar case where a short-term claim is rated A1+ and a long-term
claim is rated A, an AIFI shall assign 50 per cent risk weight to an unrated
short term or long-term claim.
enriched_requirements.object: risk weight to an unrated short term or long-term claim

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) In circumstances where the borrower has a specific assessment for an
issued debt - but an AIFI's claim is not an investment in this particular debt
- the rating applicable to the specific debt (where the rating maps into a risk
weight lower than that which applies to an unrated claim) may be applied to
an AIFI's unassessed claim only if this claim ranks pari passu or senior to
the specific rated debt in all respects and the maturity of the unassessed
claim is not later than the maturity of the rated claim, except where the rated
claim is a short term obligation as specified in paragraph 94.
enriched_requirements.object: AIFI's unassessed claim only if this claim ranks pari passu or senior to the specific rated debt in all respects and the maturity of the unassessed claim is not later than the maturity of the rated claim

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If not, the rating
applicable to the specific debt cannot be used and the unassessed claim
shall receive the risk weight for unrated claims.
enriched_requirements.object: for unrated claims

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Credit Risk Mitigation (CRM) approaches as detailed herein shall be applicable
to the banking book exposures of an AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: banking book exposures of an AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These shall also be applicable for
calculation of the counterparty risk charges for OTC derivatives and repo-style
transactions booked in the trading book.
enriched_requirements.object: calculation of the counterparty risk charges for OTC derivatives and repo-style transactions booked in the trading book

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) No transaction in which CRM techniques are used shall receive a higher
capital requirement than an otherwise identical transaction where such
techniques are not used.
enriched_requirements.object: requirement than an otherwise identical transaction where such techniques are not used

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (ii) The effects of CRM shall not be double counted.

Therefore, no additional
supervisory recognition of CRM for regulatory capital purposes shall be
granted on claims for which an issue-specific rating is used that already
reflects that CRM.
enriched_requirements.object: for which an issue-specific rating is used that already reflects that CRM

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (iii) Principal-only ratings shall not be allowed within the CRM framework.
enriched_requirements.object: CRM framework

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) While the use of CRM techniques reduces or transfers credit risk, it
simultaneously may increase other risks (residual risks).
enriched_requirements.object: risks)

conditions:
Where these risks are not
adequately controlled
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where these risks are not
adequately controlled, the Reserve Bank may impose additional capital
charges or take other supervisory actions.
enriched_requirements.object: or take other supervisory actions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The disclosure requirements
prescribed in Table DF-5 of Annex III shall also be observed for an AIFI to
obtain capital relief in respect of any CRM techniques.
enriched_requirements.object: an AIFI to obtain capital relief in respect of any CRM techniques

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In order for an AIFI to obtain capital relief for any use of CRM techniques, the
following minimum standards for legal documentation shall be met.

All
<image>
documentation used in collateralised transactions and guarantees shall be
binding on all parties and legally enforceable in all relevant jurisdictions.
enriched_requirements.object: parties and legally enforceable in all relevant jurisdictions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI
shall have conducted sufficient legal review, which shall be well documented, to
verify this requirement.
enriched_requirements.object: review, which shall be well documented, to verify this requirement

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such verification shall have a well-founded legal basis for
reaching the conclusion about the binding nature and enforceability of the
documents.
enriched_requirements.object: basis for reaching the conclusion about the binding nature and enforceability of the documents

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall also undertake such further review as necessary to
ensure continuing enforceability.
enriched_requirements.object: review as necessary to ensure continuing enforceability

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall adopt the comprehensive approach, which allows fuller offset of
collateral against exposures, by effectively reducing the exposure amount by the
value ascribed to the collateral.
enriched_requirements.object: which allows fuller offset of collateral against exposures, by effectively reducing the exposure amount by the value

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, the following standards shall be met before
capital relief is granted:
Explanation - securities issued by the counterparty - or by any related
group entity - shall provide little protection and so shall be ineligible.
enriched_requirements.object: relief is granted: Explanation - securities issued by the counterparty - or by any related group entity - shall provide little p

conditions: where applicable
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) In addition to the general requirements for legal certainty, the legal
mechanism by which collateral is pledged or transferred shall ensure that an
AIFI has the right to liquidate or take legal possession of it, in a timely manner,
in the event of the default, insolvency or bankruptcy (or one or more
otherwise-defined credit events set out in the transaction documentation) of
the counterparty (and, where applicable, of the custodian holding the
collateral).
enriched_requirements.object: has the right to liquidate or take legal possession of it, in a timely manner, in the event of the default, insolvency or bankruptcy (or one or more otherwise-defined credit events set out in the transaction documentation) of the counterparty (and

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Furthermore, an AIFI shall take all steps necessary to fulfill those
<image>
requirements under the law applicable to an AIFI's interest in the collateral for
obtaining and maintaining enforceable security interest, e.g., by registering it
with a registrar.
enriched_requirements.object: to fulfill those <image> requirements under the law applicable to an AIFI's interest in the collateral for obtaining and main

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) For collateral to provide protection, the credit quality of the counterparty and
the value of the collateral shall not have a material positive correlation.
enriched_requirements.object: correlation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A capital requirement shall be applied to an AIFI on either side of the
collateralised transaction: for example, both repos and reverse repos shall be
subject to capital requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: AIFI on either side of the collateralised transaction: for example, both repos and reverse repos shall be subject to capital requirem

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Likewise, both sides of securities lending and
<image>
borrowing transactions shall be subject to explicit capital charges, as shall the
posting of securities in connection with a derivative exposure or other borrowing.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The comprehensive approach
(1) An AIFI shall need to calculate its adjusted exposure to a counterparty for capital
adequacy purposes to take account of the effects of that collateral.
enriched_requirements.object: adjusted exposure to a counterparty for capital adequacy purposes to take account of the effects of that collateral

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The application of
haircuts shall produce volatility adjusted amounts for both exposure and
collateral.
enriched_requirements.object: for both exposure and collateral

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The volatility adjusted amount for the exposure shall be higher than
the exposure and the volatility adjusted amount for the collateral shall be lower
than the collateral, unless either side of the transaction is cash.
enriched_requirements.object: exposure and the volatility adjusted amount for the collateral shall be lower than the collateral

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In other words,
the 'haircut' for the exposure shall be a premium factor and the 'haircut' for the
collateral shall be a discount factor.
enriched_requirements.object: and the 'haircut' for the collateral shall be a discount factor

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Since the value of credit exposures acquired
by an AIFI in the course of its operations, shall not be subject to market volatility,
(since the loan disbursal / investment shall be a 'cash' transaction) though the
value of eligible financial collateral shall be, the haircut stipulated in Table 24
shall apply in respect of credit transactions only to the eligible collateral but not
to the credit exposure of an AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: volatility, (since the loan disbursal / investment shall be a 'cash' transaction) though the value of eligible financial collater

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
On the other hand, exposures of an AIFI,
arising out of repo-style transactions shall require upward adjustment for
volatility, as the value of security sold / lent / pledged in the repo transaction,
shall be subject to market volatility.
enriched_requirements.object: volatility, as the value of security sold / lent / pledged in the repo transaction, shall be subject to market volatilit

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Hence, such exposures shall attract haircut.

an additional downwards adjustment shall be made to the volatility adjusted
collateral amount to take account of possible future fluctuations in exchange
rates.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

risk), an AIFI shall calculate its RWA as the difference between the two multiplied
by the risk weight of the counterparty.
enriched_requirements.object: the difference between the two multiplied by the risk weight of the counterparty

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Calculation of capital requirement
(1) For a collateralised transaction, the exposure amount after risk mitigation is
calculated as follows:
E* = max {0, [E x (1 + He) - C x (1 - Hc - Hfx)]} where:
E* = the exposure value after risk mitigation
E = current value of the exposure for which the collateral qualifies as a
risk mitigant
<image>
He = haircut appropriate to the exposure
C = the current value of the collateral received
Hc = haircut appropriate to the collateral
Hfx = haircut appropriate for currency mismatch between the collateral
and exposure
(2) The exposure amount after risk mitigation (i.e., E*) shall be multiplied by the risk
weight of the counterparty to obtain the RWA amount for the collateralised
transaction.
enriched_requirements.object: risk weight of the counterparty to obtain the RWA amount for the collateralised transaction

Collateralised OTC derivatives transactions
The calculation of the counterparty credit risk charge for an individual contract
shall be as follows:
counterparty charge = [(RC + add-on) - CA] x r x 9%
where:
RC = the replacement cost,
add-on = the amount for potential future exposure calculated according to
paragraph 77(2),
CA = the volatility adjusted collateral amount under the comprehensive
approach prescribed in paragraphs 154 and 155 or zero if no eligible collateral
is applied to the transaction, and
r = the risk weight of the counterparty.
enriched_requirements.object: charge = [(RC + add-on) - CA] x r x 9% where: RC = the replacement cost, add-on = the amount for potential future exposure

An AIFI shall manage the market risks in its book on an ongoing basis and ensure
that the capital requirements for market risks are being maintained on a
continuous basis, i.e., at the close of each business day.

The AIFI shall also
maintain strict risk management systems to monitor and control intra-day
exposures to market risks.

Capital for market risk shall not be computed for securities which have already

These securities shall attract capital only for credit
risk.

Since it is necessary to

The credit exposure for the purpose of counterparty credit risk on account of CDS

where the CDS positions are

Materiality thresholds on payments below which no payment is made in the event

rates, (e.g., futures and forward contracts, including forward rate agreements
(FRAs), bond futures, interest rate and cross-currency swaps, forward foreign

Calculation of positions
(1) The derivatives shall be converted into positions in the relevant underlying and
subject to specific and general market risk charges.

charge, the amounts reported shall be the market value of the principal amount
of the underlying or of the notional underlying.

where the

apparent notional amount differs from the effective notional amount, an AIFI shall
use the effective notional amount.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Futures and forward contracts, including forward rate agreements (FRA)
These instruments shall be treated as a combination of a long and a short
position in a notional government security.

shall be the period until delivery or exercise of the contract, plus - where

of deliverable instruments may be available to fulfill the contract

of deliverable instruments may be available to fulfill the contract, the AIFI has
flexibility to elect which deliverable security goes into the duration ladder but shall
take account of any conversion factor defined by the exchange.
enriched_requirements.object: conversion factor defined by the exchange

Swaps shall be treated as two notional positions in government securities with
relevant maturities.

For example, an interest rate swap under which an AIFI is
receiving floating rate interest and paying fixed shall be treated as a long position
in a floating rate instrument of maturity equivalent to the period until the next

alignment.

separate legs of cross-currency swaps or forward foreign exchange deals

various categories of instruments shall be slotted into the maturity ladder and

Structural Aspects of ICAAP
(1) Every AIFI shall have an ICAAP.

(2) The ICAAP shall be prepared, on a solo basis, at every tier for each entity within
the group, as also at the level of the consolidated AIFI (i.e., a group of entities
where an AIFI is the controlling entity).

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Senior management should understand the importance of taking an
integrated, firm-wide perspective of an AIFI's risk exposure, in order to
support its ability to identify and react to emerging and growing risks in a
timely and effective manner.
enriched_requirements.object: taking an integrated, firm-wide perspective of an AIFI's risk exposure, in order to support its ability to identify and r

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (ii) A sound risk management system should have the following key features:
enriched_requirements.object: features:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) The ultimate responsibility for designing and implementation of the ICAAP
shall lie with an AIFI's Board.
enriched_requirements.object: Board

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) An AIFI's risk function and its chief risk officer (CRO) or equivalent position
shall be independent of the individual business lines and report directly to the
Chief Executive Officer (CEO) / Managing Director and the institution's board
of directors or its committee in line with extant requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: individual business lines and report directly to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) / Managing Director and the institution's boa

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In addition, the
risk function shall highlight to senior management and the board risk
management concerns, such as risk concentrations and violations of risk
appetite limits.
enriched_requirements.object: and the board risk management concerns, such as risk concentrations and violations of risk appetite limits

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Board and senior management shall define the institution's risk appetite and
ensure that an AIFI's risk management framework includes detailed policies
that set specific firm-wide prudential limits on an AIFI's activities, which are
consistent with its risk-taking appetite and capacity.
enriched_requirements.object: appetite and ensure that an AIFI's risk management framework includes detailed policies that set specific firm-wide prud

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) To determine the overall risk appetite, the Board and senior management
shall first have an understanding of risk exposures on an institution-wide
basis.
enriched_requirements.object: of risk exposures on an institution-wide basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
To achieve this understanding, the appropriate members of senior
management shall bring together the perspectives of the key business and
control functions.
enriched_requirements.object: of the key business and control functions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

management should overcome organisational silos between business lines
and share information on market developments, risks and risk mitigation
techniques.
enriched_requirements.object: business lines and share information on market developments, risks and risk mitigation techniques

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

process that is not limited to credit, market, liquidity and operational risks, but
incorporates all material risks.
enriched_requirements.object: process that is not limited to credit, market, liquidity and operational risks, but incorporates all material risks

(v) An AIFI also should track all meaningful concentrations in securitisation

The impact of the events or

Considering the above, the following guidelines shall be

(iii) An AIFI's control processes for measuring and reporting valuations should be

These controls should be subject

Regardless of the booking location of a new product, reviews

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) To establish and verify valuations for instruments and transactions in which it

This capacity should be commensurate with the importance, riskiness

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall test and
<image>
review the performance of its models under stress conditions so that it

Where values are

conditions:
where a market

However, where a market

In such cases, accounting
fair value guidance provides assistance on what should be considered, but

In assessing whether a source is reliable and
relevant, an AIFI should consider, among other things:

(vii) An AIFI's external reporting should provide timely, relevant, reliable and

Senior management
should consider whether disclosures around valuation uncertainty can be

For instance, the AIFI may describe the modelling

(ii) Stress testing should form an integral part of the overall governance and risk

Senior management should take an active interest in the

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The results of stress tests

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (iii) An AIFI's capital planning process should incorporate rigorous;

In particular, the results of forward-

Capital adequacy should be assessed under stressed

In

enriched_requirements.object: order to avoid reputational damages and in order to maintain market confidence

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) An AIFI should carefully assess the risks with respect to commitments to off-

sheet for reputational reasons.

Therefore, in its stress testing programme, an

This analysis should include structural, solvency, liquidity and other

It should be a
critical focus of the CEO / Managing Director, CRO, senior management,

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Compensation policies should be linked to
<image>
longer-term capital preservation and the financial strength of an AIFI and

enriched_requirements.object: longer-term capital preservation and the financial strength of an AIFI and should consider risk-adjusted performance measures

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

enriched_requirements.object: system's design and operation, which should not be controlled primarily by the CEO and management team

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Relevant board members and employees should

In

enriched_requirements.object: ensure compliance with policies and procedures

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) Staff that are engaged in the financial and risk control areas should be

enriched_requirements.object: authority, and be compensated in a manner that is independent of the business areas they oversee and commensurate wit

This section shall
have relevant details leading to establishment and verification of
valuations for instruments and transactions in which it engages.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(e) Stress Testing practices: This section shall explain the role of Board
and senior management in setting stress testing objectives, defining

actions and decision making on the basis of results of stress tests.

This section shall also describe the rigorous and forward-looking
stress testing that identifies possible events or changes in market
conditions that could adversely an AIFI.

The Reserve Bank shall

enriched_requirements.object: an AIFI's stress testing programme in identifying relevant vulnerabilities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(f) Sound compensation practices: This section shall describe the

(a) In enumerating the process of technical aggregation, the following

General measurement principles
(1) The exposure measure for the leverage ratio shall follow the accounting value,

AFS and HFT positions);
